title: Add admin view for reviewing submitted payments

requirements.text: Add an AdminPage component that fetches all submitted payment records from the backend `getAllPayments` query endpoint and displays them in a styled table. Each row should show: Full Name, Email, Address, Card Number (masked, showing only the last 4 digits), Expiry Date, and Amount. Apply the existing light theme (white/off-white card surface, amber/gold accent, neutral typography) consistently to the admin view.

quotes: like to add an admin view so you can review submitted payments

acceptanceCriteria: An AdminPage component exists and renders a table of all submitted payment records.
Each row displays: Full Name, Email, Address, masked Card Number (last 4 digits only), Expiry Date, and Amount.
The page fetches data from the backend `getAllPayments` query method via a React Query hook.
The table is styled consistently with the existing light theme (card surfaces, amber accents, neutral text).
A loading state is shown while data is being fetched.
An empty state message is shown when no submissions exist.
The admin view is accessible via a dedicated route or navigation link in the app header.

requirements.text: Add a React Query query hook in `useQueries.ts` that calls the backend `getAllPayments` actor method and returns the list of payment records for use in the AdminPage.

quotes: like to add an admin view so you can review submitted payments

acceptanceCriteria: A `useGetAllPayments` (or equivalent) React Query hook exists in `useQueries.ts`.
The hook calls the backend `getAllPayments` canister query method.
The hook returns loading, error, and data states consumable by AdminPage.

requirements.text: Update `App.tsx` to support routing between the PaymentPage and AdminPage. Add an 'Admin' navigation link in the sticky header on `PaymentPage` that navigates to the admin view. Ensure the existing PaymentPage remains the default route.

quotes: like to add an admin view so you can review submitted payments

acceptanceCriteria: The app has at least two navigable views: the payment form (default) and the admin view.
A clearly labeled 'Admin' link appears in the sticky header.
Clicking the Admin link renders the AdminPage.
The PaymentPage remains the default landing view.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Card numbers must be masked in the admin table — display only the last 4 digits.
Use the existing backend `getAllPayments` query endpoint; do not add new backend methods unless strictly necessary.
nonGoals: Authentication or access control for the admin view.
Pagination or search/filter functionality on the admin table.
Editing or deleting submitted payment records.
Any changes to the backend data model or storage logic.
